methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
ACSL is a public-stage drone and autonomous systems company listed on the Tokyo exchange, founded by Satoshi Washiya. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains five identifiable private peers—Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Terra Drone—and no public comparables with sufficient overlap in stage or valuation tier.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The analysis identified only four same-sector peers total, but none met the filtering threshold of three or more datapoints when screened for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier characteristics. A broad all-sector median approach would introduce excessive noise and obscure meaningful valuation signals specific to frontier autonomous systems.

Without sufficient narrow peer triangulation, this analysis cannot reliably generate a point estimate or narrow range. The key constraint is the fragmentation of the Drones & Autonomous space—few companies have reached public listing status or comparable scale, and those that have differ substantially in business model, technology maturity, and revenue stage. Any valuation conclusion would require either: (1) expansion of the comp universe to include broader mobility or robotics peers with documented loss of specificity, or (2) application of DCF or revenue multiple methods using ACSL's specific financials. The primary uncertainty is the absence of direct-peer benchmarking at ACSL's public-market stage.
